title: notelaea ovata
intro: Notelaea ovata, also known as the oval-leaved notelaea or the white notelaea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Olacaceae. Notelaea ovata is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Australian Aboriginal culture, where its leaves and stems are used to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Notelaea ovata include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Notelaea ovata typically involve infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
